At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style, facing south and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki (sacred fence)|ja|玉垣}}. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as {{ill|Kinensai (Prayer Festival)|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large {{ill|Camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}} and other significant trees like {{ill|Cryptomeria (sugi)|ja|スギ}} and {{ill|Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)|ja|カヤ}}. == Access == Located near {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|おおつちみおやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Otsuchi Mioya Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[ja:大土御祖神社]][[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]]

{{Shrine |name= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine & Uji no Nuki Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.2 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.1 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Kunitama|lt=Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国玉命}}, Mizu Sasara Hiko-no-Mikoto, Mizu Sasara Hime-no-Mikoto
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Takamizukami |rank= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} Subordinate Shrine
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |other_names= Chaya no Mon-san (茶屋ノモンサン) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭}} (May) |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} '''Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine''' (大土御祖神社, *Ōtsuchi Mioya Jinja*) is a {{ill|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}). This article also covers '''Uji no Nuki Shrine''' (宇治乃奴鬼神社, *Uji no Nuki Jinja*), which is co-located with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[ja:大土御祖神社]] == Overview == Located at 2132 Osaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.260 near {{ill|Isuzu Bridge|ja|五十鈴橋}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106 The {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}} flows to the north of the shrine grounds.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.261 Deeper into the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社}}, one will find {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori" (Tea House Forest). At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style, facing south and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki (sacred fence)|ja|玉垣}}. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as {{ill|Kinensai (Prayer Festival)|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large {{ill|Camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}} and other significant trees like {{ill|Cryptomeria (sugi)|ja|スギ}} and {{ill|Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)|ja|カヤ}}. == Access == Located near {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|おおつちみおやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Otsuchi Mioya Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[ja:大土御祖神社]][[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qqqq]]

At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style, facing south and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki (sacred fence)|ja|玉垣}}. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as {{ill|Kinensai (Prayer Festival)|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large {{ill|Camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}} and other significant trees like {{ill|Cryptomeria (sugi)|ja|スギ}} and {{ill|Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)|ja|カヤ}}. == Access == Located near {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|おおつちみおやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Otsuchi Mioya Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[ja:大土御祖神社]][[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qqqq]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "皇大神宮}}" to "皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}"
revision.text:
{{Shrine |name= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine & Uji no Nuki Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.2 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.1 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Kunitama|lt=Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国玉命}}, Mizu Sasara Hiko-no-Mikoto, Mizu Sasara Hime-no-Mikoto
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Takamizukami |rank= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} Subordinate Shrine
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |other_names= Chaya no Mon-san (茶屋ノモンサン) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭}} (May) |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} '''Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine''' (大土御祖神社, *Ōtsuchi Mioya Jinja*) is a {{ill|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}). This article also covers '''Uji no Nuki Shrine''' (宇治乃奴鬼神社, *Uji no Nuki Jinja*), which is co-located with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[ja:大土御祖神社]] == Overview == Located at 2132 Osaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.260 near {{ill|Isuzu Bridge|ja|五十鈴橋}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106 The {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}} flows to the north of the shrine grounds.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.261 Deeper into the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社}}, one will find {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori" (Tea House Forest). At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style, facing south and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki (sacred fence)|ja|玉垣}}. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as {{ill|Kinensai (Prayer Festival)|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large {{ill|Camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}} and other significant trees like {{ill|Cryptomeria (sugi)|ja|スギ}} and {{ill|Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)|ja|カヤ}}. == Access == Located near {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|おおつちみおやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Otsuchi Mioya Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[ja:大土御祖神社]][[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qqqq]]

At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style, facing south and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki (sacred fence)|ja|玉垣}}. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as {{ill|Kinensai (Prayer Festival)|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large {{ill|Camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}} and other significant trees like {{ill|Cryptomeria (sugi)|ja|スギ}} and {{ill|Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)|ja|カヤ}}. == Access == Located near {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|おおつちみおやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Otsuchi Mioya Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[ja:大土御祖神社]][[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qqqq]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 3]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Categoery:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Shrine |name= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine & Uji no Nuki Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.2 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.1 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Kunitama|lt=Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国玉命}}, Mizu Sasara Hiko-no-Mikoto, Mizu Sasara Hime-no-Mikoto
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Takamizukami |rank= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} Subordinate Shrine
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |other_names= Chaya no Mon-san (茶屋ノモンサン) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭}} (May) |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} '''Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine''' (大土御祖神社, *Ōtsuchi Mioya Jinja*) is a {{ill|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}). This article also covers '''Uji no Nuki Shrine''' (宇治乃奴鬼神社, *Uji no Nuki Jinja*), which is co-located with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[ja:大土御祖神社]] == Overview == Located at 2132 Osaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.260 near {{ill|Isuzu Bridge|ja|五十鈴橋}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106 The {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}} flows to the north of the shrine grounds.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.261 Deeper into the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社}}, one will find {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori" (Tea House Forest). At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style, facing south and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki (sacred fence)|ja|玉垣}}. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as {{ill|Kinensai (Prayer Festival)|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large {{ill|Camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}} and other significant trees like {{ill|Cryptomeria (sugi)|ja|スギ}} and {{ill|Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)|ja|カヤ}}. == Access == Located near {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|おおつちみおやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Categoery:qq]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Otsuchi Mioya Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[ja:大土御祖神社]][[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qqqq]]

[[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[ja:大土御祖神社]] == Overview == Located at 2132 Osaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.260 near {{ill|Isuzu Bridge|ja|五十鈴橋}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106 The {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}} flows to the north of the shrine grounds.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.261 Deeper into the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社}}, one will find {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori" (Tea House Forest). At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style, facing south and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki (sacred fence)|ja|玉垣}}. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as {{ill|Kinensai (Prayer Festival)|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large {{ill|Camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}} and other significant trees like {{ill|Cryptomeria (sugi)|ja|スギ}} and {{ill|Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)|ja|カヤ}}. == Access == Located near {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|おおつちみおやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category: :qq]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Otsuchi Mioya Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[ja:大土御祖神社]][[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:New]]

{{Shrine |name= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine & Uji no Nuki Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.2 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.1 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Kunitama|lt=Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国玉命}}, Mizu Sasara Hiko-no-Mikoto, Mizu Sasara Hime-no-Mikoto
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Takamizukami |rank= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} Subordinate Shrine
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |other_names= Chaya no Mon-san (茶屋ノモンサン) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭}} (May) |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} '''Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine''' (大土御祖神社, *Ōtsuchi Mioya Jinja*) is a {{ill|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}). This article also covers '''Uji no Nuki Shrine''' (宇治乃奴鬼神社, *Uji no Nuki Jinja*), which is co-located with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style, facing south and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki (sacred fence)|ja|玉垣}}. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[ja:大土御祖神社]] == Overview == Located at 2132 Osaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.260 near {{ill|Isuzu Bridge|ja|五十鈴橋}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106 The {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}} flows to the north of the shrine grounds.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.261 Deeper into the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社}}, one will find {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori" (Tea House Forest). At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style, facing south and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki (sacred fence)|ja|玉垣}}. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as {{ill|Kinensai (Prayer Festival)|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large {{ill|Camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}} and other significant trees like {{ill|Cryptomeria (sugi)|ja|スギ}} and {{ill|Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)|ja|カヤ}}. == Access == Located near {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|おおつちみおやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Otsuchi Mioya Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[ja:大土御祖神社]][[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:New]]

{{Shrine |name= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine & Uji no Nuki Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.2 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.1 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Kunitama|lt=Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国玉命}}, Mizu Sasara Hiko-no-Mikoto, Mizu Sasara Hime-no-Mikoto
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Takamizukami |rank= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} Subordinate Shrine
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |other_names= Chaya no Mon-san (茶屋ノモンサン) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭}} (May) |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} '''Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine''' (大土御祖神社, *Ōtsuchi Mioya Jinja*) is a {{ill|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}). This article also covers '''Uji no Nuki Shrine''' (宇治乃奴鬼神社, *Uji no Nuki Jinja*), which is co-located with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[ja:大土御祖神社]] == Overview == Located at 2132 Osaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.260 near {{ill|Isuzu Bridge|ja|五十鈴橋}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106 The {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}} flows to the north of the shrine grounds.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.261 Deeper into the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社}}, one will find {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori" (Tea House Forest). At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style, facing south and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki (sacred fence)|ja|玉垣}}. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as {{ill|Kinensai (Prayer Festival)|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large {{ill|Camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}} and other significant trees like {{ill|Cryptomeria (sugi)|ja|スギ}} and {{ill|Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)|ja|カヤ}}. == Access == Located near {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. This article also covers '''Uji no Nuki Shrine''' (宇治乃奴鬼神社, *Uji no Nuki Jinja*), which is co-located with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[ja:大土御祖神社]] == Overview == Located at 2132 Osaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.260 near {{ill|Isuzu Bridge|ja|五十鈴橋}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106 The {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}} flows to the north of the shrine grounds.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.261 Deeper into the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社}}, one will find {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori" (Tea House Forest). At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style, facing south and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki (sacred fence)|ja|玉垣}}. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as {{ill|Kinensai (Prayer Festival)|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large {{ill|Camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}} and other significant trees like {{ill|Cryptomeria (sugi)|ja|スギ}} and {{ill|Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)|ja|カヤ}}. == Access == Located near {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|おおつちみおやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[ja:大土御祖神社]][[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:New]]

This article also covers '''Uji no Nuki Shrine''' (宇治乃奴鬼神社, *Uji no Nuki Jinja*), which is co-located with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[ja:大土御祖神社]] == Overview == Located at 2132 Osaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.260 near {{ill|Isuzu Bridge|ja|五十鈴橋}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106 The {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}} flows to the north of the shrine grounds.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.261 Deeper into the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社}}, one will find {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori" (Tea House Forest). At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style, facing south and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki (sacred fence)|ja|玉垣}}. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as {{ill|Kinensai (Prayer Festival)|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large {{ill|Camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}} and other significant trees like {{ill|Cryptomeria (sugi)|ja|スギ}} and {{ill|Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)|ja|カヤ}}. == Access == Located near {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|おおつちみおやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[ja:大土御祖神社]][[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:New]]

Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[ja:大土御祖神社]] == Overview == Located at 2132 Osaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.260 near {{ill|Isuzu Bridge|ja|五十鈴橋}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106 The {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}} flows to the north of the shrine grounds.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.261 Deeper into the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社}}, one will find {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori" (Tea House Forest). At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style, facing south and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki (sacred fence)|ja|玉垣}}. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as {{ill|Kinensai (Prayer Festival)|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large {{ill|Camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}} and other significant trees like {{ill|Cryptomeria (sugi)|ja|スギ}} and {{ill|Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)|ja|カヤ}}. == Access == Located near {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|おおつちみおやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[ja:大土御祖神社]][[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

This article also covers '''Uji no Nuki Shrine''' (宇治乃奴鬼神社, *Uji no Nuki Jinja*), which is co-located with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style, facing south and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki (sacred fence)|ja|玉垣}}. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[ja:大土御祖神社]] == Overview == Located at 2132 Osaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.260 near {{ill|Isuzu Bridge|ja|五十鈴橋}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106 The {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}} flows to the north of the shrine grounds.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.261 Deeper into the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社}}, one will find {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori" (Tea House Forest). At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style, facing south and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki (sacred fence)|ja|玉垣}}. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as {{ill|Kinensai (Prayer Festival)|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large {{ill|Camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}} and other significant trees like {{ill|Cryptomeria (sugi)|ja|スギ}} and {{ill|Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)|ja|カヤ}}. == Access == Located near {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|おおつちみおやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[ja:大土御祖神社]][[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:goodbye $1]][[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:goodbye $1]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{Shrine |name= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine & Uji no Nuki Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.2 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.1 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Kunitama|lt=Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国玉命}}, Mizu Sasara Hiko-no-Mikoto, Mizu Sasara Hime-no-Mikoto
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Takamizukami |rank= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} Subordinate Shrine
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |other_names= Chaya no Mon-san (茶屋ノモンサン) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭}} (May) |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} '''Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine''' (大土御祖神社, *Ōtsuchi Mioya Jinja*) is a {{ill|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}). This article also covers '''Uji no Nuki Shrine''' (宇治乃奴鬼神社, *Uji no Nuki Jinja*), which is co-located with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style, facing south and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki (sacred fence)|ja|玉垣}}. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as {{ill|Kinensai (Prayer Festival)|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large {{ill|Camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}} and other significant trees like {{ill|Cryptomeria (sugi)|ja|スギ}} and {{ill|Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)|ja|カヤ}}. == Access == Located near {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. This article also covers '''Uji no Nuki Shrine''' (宇治乃奴鬼神社, *Uji no Nuki Jinja*), which is co-located with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[ja:大土御祖神社]] == Overview == Located at 2132 Osaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.260 near {{ill|Isuzu Bridge|ja|五十鈴橋}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106 The {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}} flows to the north of the shrine grounds.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.261 Deeper into the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社}}, one will find {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori" (Tea House Forest). At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style, facing south and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki (sacred fence)|ja|玉垣}}. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as {{ill|Kinensai (Prayer Festival)|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large {{ill|Camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}} and other significant trees like {{ill|Cryptomeria (sugi)|ja|スギ}} and {{ill|Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)|ja|カヤ}}. == Access == Located near {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|おおつちみおやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[ja:大土御祖神社]][[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] [[Category:SuQqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]]
