{{Shrine |name= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine & Uji no Nuki Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.2 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.1 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Kunitama|lt=Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国玉命}}, Mizu Sasara Hiko-no-Mikoto, Mizu Sasara Hime-no-Mikoto
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Takamizukami |Rank= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} Subordinate Shrine
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |other_names= Chaya no Mon-san (茶屋ノモンサン) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭}} (May) |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} '''Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine''' (大土御祖神社, *Ōtsuchi Mioya Jinja*) is a {{ill|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}). This article also covers '''Uji no Nuki Shrine''' (宇治乃奴鬼神社, *Uji no Nuki Jinja*), which is co-located with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. == Overview == Located at 2132 Osaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.260 near {{ill|Isuzu Bridge|ja|五十鈴橋}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106 The {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}} flows to the north of the shrine grounds.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.261 Deeper into the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社}}, one will find {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori" (Tea House Forest). At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style, facing south and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki (sacred fence)|ja|玉垣}}. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as {{ill|Kinensai (Prayer Festival)|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large {{ill|Camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}} and other significant trees like {{ill|Cryptomeria (sugi)|ja|スギ}} and {{ill|Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)|ja|カヤ}}. == Access == Located near {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:大土御祖神社]] [[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]]

revision.comment: 12 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:大土御祖神社 up to rev 97972585
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社 |画像= [[File:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]楠部町字尾崎2132 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 33.2 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 44 | 経度秒 = 14.1 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 大土御祖神社：[[国魂|大国玉命]]・水佐々良比古命・水佐々良比賣命
宇治乃奴鬼神社：高水上神 |社格= 大土御祖神社：式内社（小）、[[皇大神宮]]摂社
宇治乃奴鬼神社：皇大神宮末社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 茶屋ノモンサン |札所等= |例祭= [[御田植祭|御田植初]]（5月） |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''大土御祖神社'''（おおつちみおやじんじゃ）は、[[伊勢神宮]][[皇大神宮]]（内宮）の[[摂末社|摂社]]。本項目では、大土御祖神社と同座する、内宮末社の'''宇治乃奴鬼神社'''（うじのぬきじんじゃ）についても記述する。 2社とも[[倭姫命]]の定めた神社である学研パブリッシング（2013）：61ページ伊勢文化舎（2008）：106 - 107ページ。 == 概要 == [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]楠部町字尾崎2132式内社研究会 編（1990）：260ページ、五十鈴橋のそばに鎮座する伊勢文化舎（2008）：106ページ。社地の北側を五十鈴川が流れている式内社研究会 編（1990）：261ページ。大土御祖神社の参道をさらに奥へ進むと内宮摂末社の[[国津御祖神社|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社]]が鎮座する伊勢文化舎（2008）：107ページ。 社地は「茶屋の森」と呼ばれ、[[1933年]]（昭和8年）の調査では「茶屋の森さん」の転訛した「茶屋ノモンサン」と呼ばれていたという。社地の入り口とその前を通る道路の間には、幅約1.5[[メートル|m]]ほどの[[溝]]がある式内社研究会 編（1990）：265ページ。 2社は同座しており、[[社殿]]は1つである。[[神明造]][[板葺]]の社殿は[[南]]の方角を向いており、一重の[[玉垣]]に囲まれている。[[賽銭]]箱は置かれていない。社地の[[面積]]は370.87[[坪]]（≒1,226[[平方メートル|m2]]）。『皇大神宮儀式帳』によれば、[[平安時代]]の社地はこの7倍ほどの面積であったとされる。 == 大土御祖神社 == 大土御祖神社は、内宮の摂社27社のうち第10位である宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：9 - 11ページ。摂社の中で比較的上位に位置するが、これは神宮神田との関わりが深い神社であることが要因であると推察される式内社研究会 編（1990）：262ページ。 [[祭神]]は[[国魂|大国玉命]]（おおくにたまのみこと）・水佐々良比古命（みずささらひこのみこと）・水佐々良比賣命（みずささらひめのみこと）。3柱とも国生（くなり）の神の御子である。このうち、大国玉命は豊受大神宮（外宮）摂社の[[度会大国玉比賣神社]]にも祀られ、同社には漢字表記こそ異なるが、弥豆佐佐良比賣命（みずささらひめのみこと）も祀られている伊勢文化舎（2008）：37ページ。大国玉命は土地の守護神、水佐々良比古命・水佐々良比賣命は[[水神|水の神]]であるとされるが、弥豆佐々良比売命は『伊勢国風土記』逸文で[[伊勢国造]]の祖・[[天日別命]]の妻とされ、弥豆佐々良比古命は夫の天日別命と見られる。また弥豆佐々良比売命は[[伊勢津彦]]（[[武蔵国造]]等の祖）の娘とされ、比売の子・彦國見賀岐建與束命は[[度会国御神社]]の祭神とされる。『大神宮儀式解』では水佐々良比古命・水佐々良比賣命を[[夫婦]]神とする式内社研究会 編（1990）：261 - 262ページ。[[古書]]でも祭神名は一致するが、『伊勢二所太神宮神名秘書』のみ水佐々良比古命を水佐々良'''彦'''命と記す。 == 宇治乃奴鬼神社 == [[File:Koujo-no-Mori, Kusube.jpg|thumb|旧社地の「皇女の森」]] 宇治乃奴鬼神社は、内宮の末社16社のうち、第8位である宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：13 - 14ページ。旧社地は[[国道23号]]と[[近鉄鳥羽線]]の[[立体交差]]する付近、「皇女の森」と呼ばれる水田の中にある小さな森であり、「宇治乃奴鬼神社跡」の標柱がある{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html |title=斎王ゆかりの地（町外編） - 明和町役場 | publisher=三重県明和町 |language=日本語 |accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2045-21/www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html |archivedate=2013-11-03}}。 祭神は高水上命（たかみなかみのみこと）。大水上命（おおみなかみのみこと）の子であるとされ、[[灌漑用水]]の神であるという。宇治乃奴鬼神社と同じ内宮末社の[[津長神社#石井神社|石井神社]]でも同じ神を祀る伊勢文化舎（2008）：116ページ。 == 歴史 == 伊勢神宮の摂社の定義より『[[延喜式神名帳]]』成立、すなわち[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前に創建された伊勢文化舎（2008）：22ページ。『皇大神宮儀式帳』にも記載があることから[[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前から存在したことになる。 [[中世]]以後、祭祀が断絶し、[[寛文]]3年[[12月22日 (旧暦)|12月22日]]（[[グレゴリオ暦]]：[[1664年]][[1月20日]]）に[[遷御]]（せんぎょ）の儀を行い再興された。『延暦儀式帳』および『倭姫命世記』に御刀田（＝神宮神田）と家田の堰（＝楠部町尾崎）の存在が記されていたことから、旧社地を特定するのは容易であったと見られる。寛文4年10月（グレゴリオ暦：1664年11月）に楠部村年寄が[[山田奉行|山田奉行所]]に提出した[[訴状]]によれば、当時10人の[[祝部]]が任官されていた式内社研究会 編（1990）：262 - 263ページ。 社殿は[[1914年]]（大正3年）造り替えられた宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：10ページ。45年後の[[1959年]]（昭和34年）7月に建て替えられ、[[1982年]]（昭和57年）9月に大修繕が施された。 == 祭祀 == 巡回祭典の形で[[祈年祭]]（2月）、[[月次祭]]（6月・12月）、[[神嘗祭]]（10月）、[[新嘗祭]]（11月）の際は社殿の前で祭祀が行われ、[[歳旦祭]]（1月）、[[元始祭]]（1月）、建国記念祭（[[2月11日]]）、風日祈祭（5月・8月）、[[天長祭]]（[[12月23日]]）は遥祀を催行する式内社研究会 編（1990）：263ページ。 大土御祖神社のみの特殊[[神事]]として、「御田植祭」がある。[[元文]]4年（[[1739年]]）の『皇大神宮年中行事当時勤行次第』に記載のある祭儀であるが、[[古代]]の[[書物]]には見られない。[[明治4年]]（1871年）に一度中絶するが、[[大正|大正時代]]末期に再興され、当時は楠部青年会・楠部処女会が奉仕したが、後に「楠部町神宮神田御田植初祭保存会」が結成され、奉仕するようになった式内社研究会 編（1990）：264ページ。御田植初の終了後、社殿の前で[[青年]]らが「ホコリ」・「舟コギ」・「トウ舞」・「笛七折」などの[[踊り]]（田舞）を奉納する。 == 植物相 == 参道に大きな[[クスノキ]]（直径98[[センチメートル|cm]]）が茂る。ほかに[[幹]]の太い木は、[[スギ]]・[[カヤ|カヤノキ]]が挙げられる。 [[1977年]]（昭和52年）に行われた[[境内]]の[[立木]]調査によると、カヤノキ・[[イヌマキ|マキ]]・スギ・[[ヒノキ]]・[[カシ]]・[[シイ]]・クスノキ・[[ヤブニッケイ]]・[[シロダモ]]・[[タブノキ|タブ]]・[[トベラ]]・[[モチノキ]]・[[ヤブツバキ]]を主要樹種とし、計258本あった。 == 交通 == [[File:Chaya no Mori in Kusube.jpg|thumb|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社の入り口]] 大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社の東には神宮神田が広がる。付近には[[伊勢市立四郷小学校]]、伊勢市役所四郷支所、櫲樟尾神社（くすおじんじゃ）がある。 * [[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄鳥羽線|鳥羽線]][[五十鈴川駅]]より ** 徒歩約13分（約1[[キロメートル|km]]）。 ** [[伊勢市コミュニティバス]]「おかげバス」乗車、「四郷小学校前」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、徒歩約1分（約150m）。 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[伊勢インターチェンジ|伊勢IC]]より[[国道23号]]を南下、「楠部」[[交差点]]を左折、[[三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線]]を東へ約1分（約750m）。付近に[[駐車場]]はない。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * [[神宮125社の一覧]] * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html 大土御祖神社（おおつちみおやじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（宇治乃奴鬼神社の説明を含む） * [http://www.kirari1000.com/www.kirari1000.com.base_data.base_data.phpQkirari_cd=00397.html 神宮御田植え （伊勢市）] - [[伊勢志摩きらり千選]]（財団法人伊勢志摩国立公園協会） {{神宮125社}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:おおつちみおやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:神宮125社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[大土御祖神社]] to [[Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社 |画像= [[File:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]楠部町字尾崎2132 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 33.2 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 44 | 経度秒 = 14.1 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 大土御祖神社：[[国魂|大国玉命]]・水佐々良比古命・水佐々良比賣命
宇治乃奴鬼神社：高水上神 |社格= 大土御祖神社：式内社（小）、[[皇大神宮]]摂社
宇治乃奴鬼神社：皇大神宮末社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 茶屋ノモンサン |札所等= |例祭= [[御田植祭|御田植初]]（5月） |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''大土御祖神社'''（おおつちみおやじんじゃ）は、[[伊勢神宮]][[皇大神宮]]（内宮）の[[摂末社|摂社]]。本項目では、大土御祖神社と同座する、内宮末社の'''宇治乃奴鬼神社'''（うじのぬきじんじゃ）についても記述する。 2社とも[[倭姫命]]の定めた神社である学研パブリッシング（2013）：61ページ伊勢文化舎（2008）：106 - 107ページ。 == 概要 == [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]楠部町字尾崎2132式内社研究会 編（1990）：260ページ、五十鈴橋のそばに鎮座する伊勢文化舎（2008）：106ページ。社地の北側を五十鈴川が流れている式内社研究会 編（1990）：261ページ。大土御祖神社の参道をさらに奥へ進むと内宮摂末社の[[国津御祖神社|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社]]が鎮座する伊勢文化舎（2008）：107ページ。 社地は「茶屋の森」と呼ばれ、[[1933年]]（昭和8年）の調査では「茶屋の森さん」の転訛した「茶屋ノモンサン」と呼ばれていたという。社地の入り口とその前を通る道路の間には、幅約1.5[[メートル|m]]ほどの[[溝]]がある式内社研究会 編（1990）：265ページ。 2社は同座しており、[[社殿]]は1つである。[[神明造]][[板葺]]の社殿は[[南]]の方角を向いており、一重の[[玉垣]]に囲まれている。[[賽銭]]箱は置かれていない。社地の[[面積]]は370.87[[坪]]（≒1,226[[平方メートル|m2]]）。『皇大神宮儀式帳』によれば、[[平安時代]]の社地はこの7倍ほどの面積であったとされる。 == 大土御祖神社 == 大土御祖神社は、内宮の摂社27社のうち第10位である宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：9 - 11ページ。摂社の中で比較的上位に位置するが、これは神宮神田との関わりが深い神社であることが要因であると推察される式内社研究会 編（1990）：262ページ。 [[祭神]]は[[国魂|大国玉命]]（おおくにたまのみこと）・水佐々良比古命（みずささらひこのみこと）・水佐々良比賣命（みずささらひめのみこと）。3柱とも国生（くなり）の神の御子である。このうち、大国玉命は豊受大神宮（外宮）摂社の[[度会大国玉比賣神社]]にも祀られ、同社には漢字表記こそ異なるが、弥豆佐佐良比賣命（みずささらひめのみこと）も祀られている伊勢文化舎（2008）：37ページ。大国玉命は土地の守護神、水佐々良比古命・水佐々良比賣命は[[水神|水の神]]であるとされるが、弥豆佐々良比売命は『伊勢国風土記』逸文で[[伊勢国造]]の祖・[[天日別命]]の妻とされ、弥豆佐々良比古命は夫の天日別命と見られる。また弥豆佐々良比売命は[[伊勢津彦]]（[[武蔵国造]]等の祖）の娘とされ、比売の子・彦國見賀岐建與束命は[[度会国御神社]]の祭神とされる。『大神宮儀式解』では水佐々良比古命・水佐々良比賣命を[[夫婦]]神とする式内社研究会 編（1990）：261 - 262ページ。[[古書]]でも祭神名は一致するが、『伊勢二所太神宮神名秘書』のみ水佐々良比古命を水佐々良'''彦'''命と記す。 == 宇治乃奴鬼神社 == [[File:Koujo-no-Mori, Kusube.jpg|thumb|旧社地の「皇女の森」]] 宇治乃奴鬼神社は、内宮の末社16社のうち、第8位である宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：13 - 14ページ。旧社地は[[国道23号]]と[[近鉄鳥羽線]]の[[立体交差]]する付近、「皇女の森」と呼ばれる水田の中にある小さな森であり、「宇治乃奴鬼神社跡」の標柱がある{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html |title=斎王ゆかりの地（町外編） - 明和町役場 | publisher=三重県明和町 |language=日本語 |accessdate=2013-11-03|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2045-21/www.town.meiwa.mie.jp/romantic/saio/saio4.html |archivedate=2013-11-03}}。 祭神は高水上命（たかみなかみのみこと）。大水上命（おおみなかみのみこと）の子であるとされ、[[灌漑用水]]の神であるという。宇治乃奴鬼神社と同じ内宮末社の[[津長神社#石井神社|石井神社]]でも同じ神を祀る伊勢文化舎（2008）：116ページ。 == 歴史 == 伊勢神宮の摂社の定義より『[[延喜式神名帳]]』成立、すなわち[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前に創建された伊勢文化舎（2008）：22ページ。『皇大神宮儀式帳』にも記載があることから[[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前から存在したことになる。 [[中世]]以後、祭祀が断絶し、[[寛文]]3年[[12月22日 (旧暦)|12月22日]]（[[グレゴリオ暦]]：[[1664年]][[1月20日]]）に[[遷御]]（せんぎょ）の儀を行い再興された。『延暦儀式帳』および『倭姫命世記』に御刀田（＝神宮神田）と家田の堰（＝楠部町尾崎）の存在が記されていたことから、旧社地を特定するのは容易であったと見られる。寛文4年10月（グレゴリオ暦：1664年11月）に楠部村年寄が[[山田奉行|山田奉行所]]に提出した[[訴状]]によれば、当時10人の[[祝部]]が任官されていた式内社研究会 編（1990）：262 - 263ページ。 社殿は[[1914年]]（大正3年）造り替えられた宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：10ページ。45年後の[[1959年]]（昭和34年）7月に建て替えられ、[[1982年]]（昭和57年）9月に大修繕が施された。 == 祭祀 == 巡回祭典の形で[[祈年祭]]（2月）、[[月次祭]]（6月・12月）、[[神嘗祭]]（10月）、[[新嘗祭]]（11月）の際は社殿の前で祭祀が行われ、[[歳旦祭]]（1月）、[[元始祭]]（1月）、建国記念祭（[[2月11日]]）、風日祈祭（5月・8月）、[[天長祭]]（[[12月23日]]）は遥祀を催行する式内社研究会 編（1990）：263ページ。 大土御祖神社のみの特殊[[神事]]として、「御田植祭」がある。[[元文]]4年（[[1739年]]）の『皇大神宮年中行事当時勤行次第』に記載のある祭儀であるが、[[古代]]の[[書物]]には見られない。[[明治4年]]（1871年）に一度中絶するが、[[大正|大正時代]]末期に再興され、当時は楠部青年会・楠部処女会が奉仕したが、後に「楠部町神宮神田御田植初祭保存会」が結成され、奉仕するようになった式内社研究会 編（1990）：264ページ。御田植初の終了後、社殿の前で[[青年]]らが「ホコリ」・「舟コギ」・「トウ舞」・「笛七折」などの[[踊り]]（田舞）を奉納する。 == 植物相 == 参道に大きな[[クスノキ]]（直径98[[センチメートル|cm]]）が茂る。ほかに[[幹]]の太い木は、[[スギ]]・[[カヤ|カヤノキ]]が挙げられる。 [[1977年]]（昭和52年）に行われた[[境内]]の[[立木]]調査によると、カヤノキ・[[イヌマキ|マキ]]・スギ・[[ヒノキ]]・[[カシ]]・[[シイ]]・クスノキ・[[ヤブニッケイ]]・[[シロダモ]]・[[タブノキ|タブ]]・[[トベラ]]・[[モチノキ]]・[[ヤブツバキ]]を主要樹種とし、計258本あった。 == 交通 == [[File:Chaya no Mori in Kusube.jpg|thumb|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社の入り口]] 大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社の東には神宮神田が広がる。付近には[[伊勢市立四郷小学校]]、伊勢市役所四郷支所、櫲樟尾神社（くすおじんじゃ）がある。 * [[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄鳥羽線|鳥羽線]][[五十鈴川駅]]より ** 徒歩約13分（約1[[キロメートル|km]]）。 ** [[伊勢市コミュニティバス]]「おかげバス」乗車、「四郷小学校前」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、徒歩約1分（約150m）。 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[伊勢インターチェンジ|伊勢IC]]より[[国道23号]]を南下、「楠部」[[交差点]]を左折、[[三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線]]を東へ約1分（約750m）。付近に[[駐車場]]はない。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * [[神宮125社の一覧]] * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html 大土御祖神社（おおつちみおやじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（宇治乃奴鬼神社の説明を含む） * [http://www.kirari1000.com/www.kirari1000.com.base_data.base_data.phpQkirari_cd=00397.html 神宮御田植え （伊勢市）] - [[伊勢志摩きらり千選]]（財団法人伊勢志摩国立公園協会） {{神宮125社}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:おおつちみおやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:神宮125社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine & Uji no Nuki Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.2 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.1 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Kunitama|lt=Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国玉命}}, Mizu Sasara Hiko-no-Mikoto, Mizu Sasara Hime-no-Mikoto
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Takamizukami |Rank= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} Subordinate Shrine
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |other_names= Chaya no Mon-san (茶屋ノモンサン) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭}} (May) |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} '''Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine''' (大土御祖神社, *Ōtsuchi Mioya Jinja*) is a {{ill|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}). This article also covers '''Uji no Nuki Shrine''' (宇治乃奴鬼神社, *Uji no Nuki Jinja*), which is co-located with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. == Overview == Located at 2132 Osaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.260 near {{ill|Isuzu Bridge|ja|五十鈴橋}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106 The {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}} flows to the north of the shrine grounds.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.261 Deeper into the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社}}, one will find {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori" (Tea House Forest). At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style, facing south and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki (sacred fence)|ja|玉垣}}. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as {{ill|Kinensai (Prayer Festival)|ja|祈年祭}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)|ja|神嘗祭}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)|ja|新嘗祭}} (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large {{ill|Camphor trees|ja|クスノキ}} and other significant trees like {{ill|Cryptomeria (sugi)|ja|スギ}} and {{ill|Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)|ja|カヤ}}. == Access == Located near {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:大土御祖神社]] [[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|大土御祖神社|version=97972585|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Shrine |name= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine & Uji no Nuki Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}, Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.2 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.1 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Kunitama|lt=Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国玉命|12=draft}}, Mizu Sasara Hiko-no-Mikoto, Mizu Sasara Hime-no-Mikoto
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Takamizukami |Rank= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} Subordinate Shrine
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} |other_names= Chaya no Mon-san (茶屋ノモンサン) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|12=draft}} (May) |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} }} '''Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine''' (大土御祖神社, *Ōtsuchi Mioya Jinja*) is a {{ill|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}). This article also covers '''Uji no Nuki Shrine''' (宇治乃奴鬼神社, *Uji no Nuki Jinja*), which is co-located with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. == Overview == Located at 2132 Osaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.260 near {{ill|Isuzu Bridge|ja|五十鈴橋|12=draft}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106 The {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川|12=draft}} flows to the north of the shrine grounds.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.261 Deeper into the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社|12=draft}}, one will find {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社|12=draft}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori" (Tea House Forest). At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿|12=draft}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} style, facing south and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki (sacred fence)|ja|玉垣|12=draft}}. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱|12=draft}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田|12=draft}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命|12=draft}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命|12=draft}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as {{ill|Kinensai (Prayer Festival)|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft}} (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large {{ill|Camphor trees|ja|クスノキ|12=draft}} and other significant trees like {{ill|Cryptomeria (sugi)|ja|スギ|12=draft}} and {{ill|Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)|ja|カヤ|12=draft}}. == Access == Located near {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|12=draft}}: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス|12=draft}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=draft}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|12=draft}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|12=draft}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|大土御祖神社|version=97972585|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:大土御祖神社]] [[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Category refresh]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Shrine |name= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine & Uji no Nuki Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft}}, Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.2 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.1 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Kunitama|lt=Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国玉命|qq=draft}}, Mizu Sasara Hiko-no-Mikoto, Mizu Sasara Hime-no-Mikoto
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Takamizukami |Rank= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft}} Subordinate Shrine
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft}} |other_names= Chaya no Mon-san (茶屋ノモンサン) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft}} (May) |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft}} }} '''Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine''' (大土御祖神社, *Ōtsuchi Mioya Jinja*) is a {{ill|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft}} (the Inner Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft}}). This article also covers '''Uji no Nuki Shrine''' (宇治乃奴鬼神社, *Uji no Nuki Jinja*), which is co-located with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. == Overview == Located at 2132 Osaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.260 near {{ill|Isuzu Bridge|ja|五十鈴橋|qq=draft}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106 The {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川|qq=draft}} flows to the north of the shrine grounds.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.261 Deeper into the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社|qq=draft}}, one will find {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社|qq=draft}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori" (Tea House Forest). At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft}} style, facing south and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki (sacred fence)|ja|玉垣|qq=draft}}. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田|qq=draft}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命|qq=draft}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命|qq=draft}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as {{ill|Kinensai (Prayer Festival)|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft}} (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large {{ill|Camphor trees|ja|クスノキ|qq=draft}} and other significant trees like {{ill|Cryptomeria (sugi)|ja|スギ|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)|ja|カヤ|qq=draft}}. == Access == Located near {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|qq=draft}}: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス|qq=draft}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|qq=draft}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|qq=draft}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|大土御祖神社|version=97972585|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:大土御祖神社]] [[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|式内社" to "|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft"

Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|大土御祖神社|version=97972585|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:大土御祖神社]] [[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]]

revision.comment: Bot: add simplewiki draft link to ill templates
revision.text:
{{Shrine |name= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine & Uji no Nuki Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise, Mie|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}, Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.2 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.1 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Kunitama|lt=Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国玉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=}}, Mizu Sasara Hiko-no-Mikoto, Mizu Sasara Hime-no-Mikoto
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Takamizukami |Rank= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai-sha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} Subordinate Shrine
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} |other_names= Chaya no Mon-san (茶屋ノモンサン) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (May) |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} }} '''Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine''' (大土御祖神社, *Ōtsuchi Mioya Jinja*) is a {{ill|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|ja|摂末社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|qq=}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (the Inner Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}). This article also covers '''Uji no Nuki Shrine''' (宇治乃奴鬼神社, *Uji no Nuki Jinja*), which is co-located with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. == Overview == Located at 2132 Osaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.260 near {{ill|Isuzu Bridge|ja|五十鈴橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzu Bridge|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106 The {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzu River|qq=}} flows to the north of the shrine grounds.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.261 Deeper into the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|qq=}}, one will find {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori" (Tea House Forest). At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden (main shrine building)|qq=}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} style, facing south and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki (sacred fence)|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagaki (sacred fence)|qq=}}. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisenbako (offertory box)|qq=}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|qq=}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|qq=}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamina Kami|qq=}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as {{ill|Kinensai (Prayer Festival)|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinensai (Prayer Festival)|qq=}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)|qq=}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)|qq=}} (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large {{ill|Camphor trees|ja|クスノキ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Camphor trees|qq=}} and other significant trees like {{ill|Cryptomeria (sugi)|ja|スギ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cryptomeria (sugi)|qq=}} and {{ill|Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)|ja|カヤ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)|qq=}}. == Access == Located near {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzugawa Station|qq=}}: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City Community Bus|qq=}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|qq=}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|qq=}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|qq=}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|大土御祖神社|version=97972585|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:大土御祖神社]] [[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]]

Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|qq=}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|大土御祖神社|version=97972585|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:大土御祖神社]] [[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] to [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Shrine |name= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine & Uji no Nuki Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise, Mie|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}, Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.2 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.1 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Kunitama|lt=Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国玉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=}}, Mizu Sasara Hiko-no-Mikoto, Mizu Sasara Hime-no-Mikoto
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Takamizukami |Rank= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} Subordinate Shrine
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} |other_names= Chaya no Mon-san (茶屋ノモンサン) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (May) |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} }} '''Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine''' (大土御祖神社, *Ōtsuchi Mioya Jinja*) is a {{ill|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|ja|摂末社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|qq=}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (the Inner Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}). This article also covers '''Uji no Nuki Shrine''' (宇治乃奴鬼神社, *Uji no Nuki Jinja*), which is co-located with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. == Overview == Located at 2132 Osaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.260 near {{ill|Isuzu Bridge|ja|五十鈴橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzu Bridge|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106 The {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzu River|qq=}} flows to the north of the shrine grounds.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.261 Deeper into the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|qq=}}, one will find {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori" (Tea House Forest). At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden (main shrine building)|qq=}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} style, facing south and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki (sacred fence)|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagaki (sacred fence)|qq=}}. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisenbako (offertory box)|qq=}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|qq=}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|qq=}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamina Kami|qq=}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as {{ill|Kinensai (Prayer Festival)|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinensai (Prayer Festival)|qq=}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)|qq=}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)|qq=}} (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large {{ill|Camphor trees|ja|クスノキ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Camphor trees|qq=}} and other significant trees like {{ill|Cryptomeria (sugi)|ja|スギ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cryptomeria (sugi)|qq=}} and {{ill|Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)|ja|カヤ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)|qq=}}. == Access == Located near {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzugawa Station|qq=}}: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City Community Bus|qq=}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|qq=}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|qq=}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|qq=}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|大土御祖神社|version=97972585|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:大土御祖神社]] [[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Engishiki Jinmyocho" to "Engishiki Jinmyōchō"

History, Volume 1*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|qq=}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|大土御祖神社|version=97972585|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:大土御祖神社]] [[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]]

revision.comment: Bot: fix redirect [[Kannamesai]] → [[Kannamesai Festival]]
revision.text:
{{Shrine |name= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine & Uji no Nuki Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise, Mie|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}, Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.2 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.1 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Kunitama|lt=Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国玉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=}}, Mizu Sasara Hiko-no-Mikoto, Mizu Sasara Hime-no-Mikoto
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Takamizukami |Rank= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} Subordinate Shrine
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} |other_names= Chaya no Mon-san (茶屋ノモンサン) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (May) |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} }} '''Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine''' (大土御祖神社, *Ōtsuchi Mioya Jinja*) is a {{ill|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|ja|摂末社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|qq=}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (the Inner Shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}). This article also covers '''Uji no Nuki Shrine''' (宇治乃奴鬼神社, *Uji no Nuki Jinja*), which is co-located with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. == Overview == Located at 2132 Osaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.260 near {{ill|Isuzu Bridge|ja|五十鈴橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzu Bridge|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106 The {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzu River|qq=}} flows to the north of the shrine grounds.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.261 Deeper into the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|qq=}}, one will find {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori" (Tea House Forest). At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden (main shrine building)|qq=}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} style, facing south and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki (sacred fence)|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagaki (sacred fence)|qq=}}. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisenbako (offertory box)|qq=}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|qq=}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|qq=}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamina Kami|qq=}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as {{ill|Kinensai (Prayer Festival)|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinensai (Prayer Festival)|qq=}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)|qq=}} (October), and {{ill|Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)|qq=}} (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large {{ill|Camphor trees|ja|クスノキ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Camphor trees|qq=}} and other significant trees like {{ill|Cryptomeria (sugi)|ja|スギ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cryptomeria (sugi)|qq=}} and {{ill|Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)|ja|カヤ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)|qq=}}. == Access == Located near {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzugawa Station|qq=}}: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City Community Bus|qq=}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|qq=}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|qq=}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|qq=}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|大土御祖神社|version=97972585|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:大土御祖神社]] [[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]]

Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|qq=}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|大土御祖神社|version=97972585|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:大土御祖神社]] [[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]]

revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
{{Shrine |name= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine & Uji no Nuki Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine.jpg|270px]] |location = [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.2 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.1 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Kunitama|lt=Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国玉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=}}, Mizu Sasara Hiko-no-Mikoto, Mizu Sasara Hime-no-Mikoto
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Takamizukami |Rank= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} Subordinate Shrine
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |other_names= Chaya no Mon-san (茶屋ノモンサン) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (May) |rituals= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] }} '''Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine''' (大土御祖神社, *Ōtsuchi Mioya Jinja*) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (the Inner Shrine of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]). This article also covers '''Uji no Nuki Shrine''' (宇治乃奴鬼神社, *Uji no Nuki Jinja*), which is co-located with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. == Overview == Located at 2132 Osaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.260 near {{ill|Isuzu Bridge|ja|五十鈴橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzu Bridge|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106 The [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]] flows to the north of the shrine grounds.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.261 Deeper into the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|qq=}}, one will find {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori" (Tea House Forest). At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden (main shrine building)|qq=}} constructed in [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style, facing south and enclosed by a single [[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki (sacred fence)]]. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisenbako (offertory box)|qq=}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|qq=}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|qq=}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamina Kami|qq=}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai (Prayer Festival)]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)]] (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large [[:en:Camphora officinarum|Camphor trees]] and other significant trees like [[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria (sugi)]] and [[:en:Torreya nucifera|Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)]]. == Access == Located near [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]]: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City Community Bus|qq=}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|qq=}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|qq=}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|qq=}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|大土御祖神社|version=97972585|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Kōtai Jingū]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:大土御祖神社]] [[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]]
