revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17222702]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine & Uji no Nuki Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine.jpg |location = [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.2 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.1 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Kunitama|lt=Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国玉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Mizu Sasara Hiko-no-Mikoto, Mizu Sasara Hime-no-Mikoto
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Takamizukami |Rank= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Subordinate Shrine
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |other_names= Chaya no Mon-san (茶屋ノモンサン) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (May) |rituals= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.475889|N|136.73725|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine''' (大土御祖神社, *Ōtsuchi Mioya Jinja*) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]). This article also covers '''Uji no Nuki Shrine''' (宇治乃奴鬼神社, *Uji no Nuki Jinja*), which is co-located with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. == Overview == Located at 2132 Osaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.260 near {{ill|Isuzu Bridge|ja|五十鈴橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzu Bridge|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106 The [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]] flows to the north of the shrine grounds.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.261 Deeper into the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, one will find {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori" (Tea House Forest). At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden (main shrine building)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} constructed in [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style, facing south and enclosed by a single [[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki (sacred fence)]]. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisenbako (offertory box)|qq=|qid=Q135491444}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamina Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai (Prayer Festival)]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)]] (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large [[:en:Camphora officinarum|Camphor trees]] and other significant trees like [[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria (sugi)]] and [[:en:Torreya nucifera|Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)]]. == Access == Located near [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]]: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City Community Bus|qq=|1=Ise City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379021}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|qq=|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 37|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357923}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Gakken|1=Gakken Plus Co., Ltd.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86741099}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|qq=|1=Minoru Sonoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622218}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|大土御祖神社|version=97972585|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:大土御祖神社]] [[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] {{Commons category|Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine}} [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17222702]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine & Uji no Nuki Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine.jpg |location = [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.2 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.1 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Kunitama|lt=Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国玉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Mizu Sasara Hiko-no-Mikoto, Mizu Sasara Hime-no-Mikoto
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Takamizukami |Rank= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Subordinate Shrine
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |other_names= Chaya no Mon-san (茶屋ノモンサン) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (May) |rituals= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.475889|N|136.73725|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine''' (大土御祖神社, *Ōtsuchi Mioya Jinja*) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]). This article also covers '''Uji no Nuki Shrine''' (宇治乃奴鬼神社, *Uji no Nuki Jinja*), which is co-located with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. == Overview == Located at 2132 Osaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.260 near {{ill|Isuzu Bridge|ja|五十鈴橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzu Bridge|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106 The [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]] flows to the north of the shrine grounds.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.261 Deeper into the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, one will find {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori" (Tea House Forest). At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden (main shrine building)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} constructed in [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style, facing south and enclosed by a single [[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki (sacred fence)]]. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisenbako (offertory box)|qq=|qid=Q135491444}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamina Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai (Prayer Festival)]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)]] (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large [[:en:Camphora officinarum|Camphor trees]] and other significant trees like [[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria (sugi)]] and [[:en:Torreya nucifera|Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)]]. == Access == Located near [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]]: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City Community Bus|qq=|1=Ise City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379021}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|qq=|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 37|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357923}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Gakken|1=Gakken Plus Co., Ltd.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86741099}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|qq=|1=Minoru Sonoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622218}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|大土御祖神社|version=97972585|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:大土御祖神社]] [[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] {{Commons category|Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine}} [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17222702]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine & Uji no Nuki Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine.jpg |location = [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.2 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.1 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Kunitama|lt=Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国玉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Mizu Sasara Hiko-no-Mikoto, Mizu Sasara Hime-no-Mikoto
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Takamizukami |Rank= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Subordinate Shrine
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |other_names= Chaya no Mon-san (茶屋ノモンサン) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (May) |rituals= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.475889|N|136.73725|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine''' (大土御祖神社, *Ōtsuchi Mioya Jinja*) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]). This article also covers '''Uji no Nuki Shrine''' (宇治乃奴鬼神社, *Uji no Nuki Jinja*), which is co-located with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. == Overview == Located at 2132 Osaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.260 near {{ill|Isuzu Bridge|ja|五十鈴橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzu Bridge|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106 The [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]] flows to the north of the shrine grounds.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.261 Deeper into the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, one will find {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori" (Tea House Forest). At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden (main shrine building)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} constructed in [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style, facing south and enclosed by a single [[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki (sacred fence)]]. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisenbako (offertory box)|qq=|qid=Q135491444}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamina Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai (Prayer Festival)]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)]] (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large [[:en:Camphora officinarum|Camphor trees]] and other significant trees like [[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria (sugi)]] and [[:en:Torreya nucifera|Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)]]. == Access == Located near [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]]: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City Community Bus|qq=|1=Ise City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379021}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|qq=|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 37|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357923}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Gakken|1=Gakken Plus Co., Ltd.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86741099}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|qq=|1=Minoru Sonoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622218}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|大土御祖神社|version=97972585|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:大土御祖神社]] [[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] {{Commons category|Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine}} [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17222702]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine & Uji no Nuki Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine.jpg |location = [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.2 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.1 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Kunitama|lt=Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国玉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Mizu Sasara Hiko-no-Mikoto, Mizu Sasara Hime-no-Mikoto
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Takamizukami |Rank= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Subordinate Shrine
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |other_names= Chaya no Mon-san (茶屋ノモンサン) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (May) |rituals= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.475889|N|136.73725|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine''' (大土御祖神社, *Ōtsuchi Mioya Jinja*) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]). This article also covers '''Uji no Nuki Shrine''' (宇治乃奴鬼神社, *Uji no Nuki Jinja*), which is co-located with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. == Overview == Located at 2132 Osaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.260 near {{ill|Isuzu Bridge|ja|五十鈴橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzu Bridge|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106 The [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]] flows to the north of the shrine grounds.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.261 Deeper into the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, one will find {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori" (Tea House Forest). At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden (main shrine building)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} constructed in [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style, facing south and enclosed by a single [[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki (sacred fence)]]. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisenbako (offertory box)|qq=|qid=Q135491444}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamina Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai (Prayer Festival)]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)]] (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large [[:en:Camphora officinarum|Camphor trees]] and other significant trees like [[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria (sugi)]] and [[:en:Torreya nucifera|Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)]]. == Access == Located near [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]]: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City Community Bus|qq=|1=Ise City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379021}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|qq=|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 37|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357923}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Gakken|1=Gakken Plus Co., Ltd.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86741099}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|qq=|1=Minoru Sonoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622218}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == References == == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|大土御祖神社|version=97972585|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]][[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:大土御祖神社]] [[sn:Otsuchi Mioya Shrine]] {{Commons category|Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine}} [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17222702]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine & Uji no Nuki Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine.jpg |location = [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.2 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.1 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Kunitama|lt=Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国玉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Mizu Sasara Hiko-no-Mikoto, Mizu Sasara Hime-no-Mikoto
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Takamizukami |Rank= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Subordinate Shrine
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |other_names= Chaya no Mon-san (茶屋ノモンサン) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (May) |rituals= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.475889|N|136.73725|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine''' (大土御祖神社, *Ōtsuchi Mioya Jinja*) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]). This article also covers '''Uji no Nuki Shrine''' (宇治乃奴鬼神社, *Uji no Nuki Jinja*), which is co-located with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. == Overview == Located at 2132 Osaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.260 near {{ill|Isuzu Bridge|ja|五十鈴橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzu Bridge|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106 The [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]] flows to the north of the shrine grounds.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.261 Deeper into the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, one will find {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori" (Tea House Forest). At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden (main shrine building)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} constructed in [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style, facing south and enclosed by a single [[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki (sacred fence)]]. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisenbako (offertory box)|qq=|qid=Q135491444}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamina Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai (Prayer Festival)]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)]] (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large [[:en:Camphora officinarum|Camphor trees]] and other significant trees like [[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria (sugi)]] and [[:en:Torreya nucifera|Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)]]. == Access == Located near [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]]: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City Community Bus|qq=|1=Ise City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379021}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|qq=|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 37|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357923}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Gakken|1=Gakken Plus Co., Ltd.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86741099}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|qq=|1=Minoru Sonoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622218}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == References == == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|大土御祖神社|version=97972585|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:大土御祖神社]] {{Commons category|Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine}} [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q17222702}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17222702]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine & Uji no Nuki Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine.jpg |location = [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.2 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.1 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Kunitama|lt=Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国玉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Mizu Sasara Hiko-no-Mikoto, Mizu Sasara Hime-no-Mikoto
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Takamizukami |Rank= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Subordinate Shrine
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |other_names= Chaya no Mon-san (茶屋ノモンサン) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (May) |rituals= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.475889|N|136.73725|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine''' (大土御祖神社, *Ōtsuchi Mioya Jinja*) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]). This article also covers '''Uji no Nuki Shrine''' (宇治乃奴鬼神社, *Uji no Nuki Jinja*), which is co-located with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. == Overview == Located at 2132 Osaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.260 near {{ill|Isuzu Bridge|ja|五十鈴橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzu Bridge|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106 The [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]] flows to the north of the shrine grounds.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.261 Deeper into the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, one will find {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori" (Tea House Forest). At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden (main shrine building)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} constructed in [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style, facing south and enclosed by a single [[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki (sacred fence)]]. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisenbako (offertory box)|qq=|qid=Q135491444}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamina Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai (Prayer Festival)]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)]] (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large [[:en:Camphora officinarum|Camphor trees]] and other significant trees like [[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria (sugi)]] and [[:en:Torreya nucifera|Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)]]. == Access == Located near [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]]: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City Community Bus|qq=|1=Ise City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379021}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|qq=|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 37|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357923}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Gakken|1=Gakken Plus Co., Ltd.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86741099}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|qq=|1=Minoru Sonoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622218}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == References == == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|大土御祖神社|version=97972585|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:大土御祖神社]] {{Commons category|Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine}} {{wikidata link|Q17222702}}

revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine]][[ja:大土御祖神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17222702]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine & Uji no Nuki Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine.jpg |location = [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.2 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.1 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Kunitama|lt=Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国玉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Mizu Sasara Hiko-no-Mikoto, Mizu Sasara Hime-no-Mikoto
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Takamizukami |Rank= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Subordinate Shrine
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |other_names= Chaya no Mon-san (茶屋ノモンサン) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (May) |rituals= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.475889|N|136.73725|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine''' (大土御祖神社, *Ōtsuchi Mioya Jinja*) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]). This article also covers '''Uji no Nuki Shrine''' (宇治乃奴鬼神社, *Uji no Nuki Jinja*), which is co-located with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. == Overview == Located at 2132 Osaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.260 near {{ill|Isuzu Bridge|ja|五十鈴橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzu Bridge|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106 The [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]] flows to the north of the shrine grounds.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.261 Deeper into the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, one will find {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori" (Tea House Forest). At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden (main shrine building)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} constructed in [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style, facing south and enclosed by a single [[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki (sacred fence)]]. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisenbako (offertory box)|qq=|qid=Q135491444}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamina Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai (Prayer Festival)]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)]] (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large [[:en:Camphora officinarum|Camphor trees]] and other significant trees like [[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria (sugi)]] and [[:en:Torreya nucifera|Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)]]. == Access == Located near [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]]: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City Community Bus|qq=|1=Ise City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379021}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|qq=|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 37|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357923}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Gakken|1=Gakken Plus Co., Ltd.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86741099}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|qq=|1=Minoru Sonoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622218}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == References == == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|大土御祖神社|version=97972585|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:大土御祖神社]] {{Commons category|Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine}} {{wikidata link|Q17222702}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[d:" to "[[da:d:"
revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine]][[ja:大土御祖神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17222702]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine & Uji no Nuki Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine.jpg |location = [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.2 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.1 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Kunitama|lt=Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国玉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Mizu Sasara Hiko-no-Mikoto, Mizu Sasara Hime-no-Mikoto
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Takamizukami |Rank= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Subordinate Shrine
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |other_names= Chaya no Mon-san (茶屋ノモンサン) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (May) |rituals= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.475889|N|136.73725|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine''' (大土御祖神社, *Ōtsuchi Mioya Jinja*) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]). This article also covers '''Uji no Nuki Shrine''' (宇治乃奴鬼神社, *Uji no Nuki Jinja*), which is co-located with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. == Overview == Located at 2132 Osaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.260 near {{ill|Isuzu Bridge|ja|五十鈴橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzu Bridge|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106 The [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]] flows to the north of the shrine grounds.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.261 Deeper into the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, one will find {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori" (Tea House Forest). At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden (main shrine building)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} constructed in [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style, facing south and enclosed by a single [[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki (sacred fence)]]. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisenbako (offertory box)|qq=|WD=Q135491444}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamina Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai (Prayer Festival)]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)]] (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large [[:en:Camphora officinarum|Camphor trees]] and other significant trees like [[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria (sugi)]] and [[:en:Torreya nucifera|Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)]]. == Access == Located near [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]]: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City Community Bus|qq=|1=Ise City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379021}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|qq=|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 37|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357923}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Gakken|1=Gakken Plus Co., Ltd.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86741099}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|qq=|1=Minoru Sonoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622218}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == References == == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|大土御祖神社|version=97972585|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:大土御祖神社]] {{Commons category|Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine}} {{wikidata link|Q17222702}}
