Uji no Nuki Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |other_names= Chaya no Mon-san (茶屋ノモンサン) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (May) |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}} |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.475889|N|136.73725|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine''' (大土御祖神社, *Ōtsuchi Mioya Jinja*) is a {{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|WD=Q1071160|lt=subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|1=Setsumatsusha}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}). This article also covers '''Uji no Nuki Shrine''' (宇治乃奴鬼神社, *Uji no Nuki Jinja*), which is co-located with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. == Overview == Located at 2132 Osaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.260 near {{ill|Isuzu Bridge|ja|五十鈴橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzu Bridge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106 The {{ill|Isuzu River|en|Isuzu River|WD=Q6089522|lt=Isuzu River|1=Isuzu River}} flows to the north of the shrine grounds.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.261 Deeper into the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, one will find {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori" (Tea House Forest). At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden (main shrine building)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} style, facing south and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=Tamagaki (sacred fence)|1=Sacred fences}}. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisenbako (offertory box)|WD=Q135491444|1=Saisenbako}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamina Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai (Prayer Festival)|1=Kinensai}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)|1=Kannamesai Festival}} (October), and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large {{ill|Camphora officinarum|en|Camphora officinarum|WD=Q158722|lt=Camphor trees|1=Camphora officinarum}} and other significant trees like {{ill|Cryptomeria|en|Cryptomeria|WD=Q147388|lt=Cryptomeria (sugi)|1=Cryptomeria}} and {{ill|Torreya nucifera|en|Torreya nucifera|WD=Q1373999|lt=Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)|1=Torreya nucifera}}. == Access == Located near {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|en|Isuzugawa Station|WD=Q6089533|lt=Isuzugawa Station|1=Isuzugawa Station}}: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City Community Bus|1=Q11379021|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379021}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 37|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357923}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Gakken|1=Gakken Plus Co., Ltd.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86741099}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|1=Minoru Sonoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622218}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == References == == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|大土御祖神社|version=97972585|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Otsuchi Mioya Shrine (Q17222702)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine & Uji no Nuki Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine.jpg |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.2 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.1 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Kunitama|lt=Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Mizu Sasara Hiko-no-Mikoto, Mizu Sasara Hime-no-Mikoto
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Takamizukami |Rank= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Subordinate Shrine
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |other_names= Chaya no Mon-san (茶屋ノモンサン) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (May) |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}} |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.475889|N|136.73725|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine''' (大土御祖神社, *Ōtsuchi Mioya Jinja*) is a {{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|WD=Q1071160|lt=subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|1=Setsumatsusha}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}). This article also covers '''Uji no Nuki Shrine''' (宇治乃奴鬼神社, *Uji no Nuki Jinja*), which is co-located with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. == Overview == Located at 2132 Osaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.260 near {{ill|Isuzu Bridge|ja|五十鈴橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzu Bridge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106 The {{ill|Isuzu River|en|Isuzu River|WD=Q6089522|lt=Isuzu River|1=Isuzu River}} flows to the north of the shrine grounds.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.261 Deeper into the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, one will find {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori" (Tea House Forest). At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden (main shrine building)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} style, facing south and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=Tamagaki (sacred fence)|1=Sacred fences}}. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisenbako (offertory box)|WD=Q135491444|1=Saisenbako}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032|1=Watarai District}} || {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|lt=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|commons|Category:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine|WD=Q17222702|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || part of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|WD=Q11581011|1=Kōtai Jingū}} || — || {{coord|34.475963|136.737142|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181131 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17222702}}==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine]] [[da:d:Q17222702]] [[ja:大土御祖神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine & Uji no Nuki Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine.jpg |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.2 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.1 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Kunitama|lt=Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Mizu Sasara Hiko-no-Mikoto, Mizu Sasara Hime-no-Mikoto
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Takamizukami |Rank= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Subordinate Shrine
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |other_names= Chaya no Mon-san (茶屋ノモンサン) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (May) |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}} |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.475889|N|136.73725|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine''' (大土御祖神社, *Ōtsuchi Mioya Jinja*) is a {{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|WD=Q1071160|lt=subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|1=Setsumatsusha}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}). This article also covers '''Uji no Nuki Shrine''' (宇治乃奴鬼神社, *Uji no Nuki Jinja*), which is co-located with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. == Overview == Located at 2132 Osaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.260 near {{ill|Isuzu Bridge|ja|五十鈴橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzu Bridge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106 The {{ill|Isuzu River|en|Isuzu River|WD=Q6089522|lt=Isuzu River|1=Isuzu River}} flows to the north of the shrine grounds.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.261 Deeper into the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, one will find {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori" (Tea House Forest). At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden (main shrine building)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} style, facing south and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=Tamagaki (sacred fence)|1=Sacred fences}}. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisenbako (offertory box)|WD=Q135491444|1=Saisenbako}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamina Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai (Prayer Festival)|1=Kinensai}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)|1=Kannamesai Festival}} (October), and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large {{ill|Camphora officinarum|en|Camphora officinarum|WD=Q158722|lt=Camphor trees|1=Camphora officinarum}} and other significant trees like {{ill|Cryptomeria|en|Cryptomeria|WD=Q147388|lt=Cryptomeria (sugi)|1=Cryptomeria}} and {{ill|Torreya nucifera|en|Torreya nucifera|WD=Q1373999|lt=Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)|1=Torreya nucifera}}. == Access == Located near {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|en|Isuzugawa Station|WD=Q6089533|lt=Isuzugawa Station|1=Isuzugawa Station}}: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City Community Bus|1=Q11379021|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379021}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 37|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357923}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Gakken|1=Gakken Plus Co., Ltd.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86741099}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|1=Minoru Sonoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622218}}, Gakken Marketing, 2013. * Shikinaisha Research Association. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6*. Kōgakkan University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka Publishing. *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, Ise Bunka Publishing, 2008. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == References == == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0305.html Otsuchi Mioya Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine * [http://www.kirari1000.com/ Ise Shima Kirari 1000] - Ise Shima National Park Association {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|大土御祖神社|version=97972585|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] '''Otsuchi Mioya Shrine (Q17222702)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032|1=Watarai District}} || {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|lt=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|commons|Category:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine|WD=Q17222702|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || part of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|WD=Q11581011|1=Kōtai Jingū}} || — || {{coord|34.475963|136.737142|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181131 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17222702}}==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine]] [[da:d:Q17222702]] [[ja:大土御祖神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine & Uji no Nuki Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Otuchi Mioya & Uji no Nuki shrine.jpg |location = {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Kusube-chō, Ōzaki 2132 |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 28 | latitude_sec = 33.2 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 44 | longitude_sec = 14.1 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Kunitama|lt=Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto|ja|大国玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Mizu Sasara Hiko-no-Mikoto, Mizu Sasara Hime-no-Mikoto
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Takamizukami |Rank= Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine: {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Subordinate Shrine
Uji no Nuki Shrine: Kōtai Jingū Associated Shrine |founded= |main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |other_names= Chaya no Mon-san (茶屋ノモンサン) |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Festival|qq=}} (May) |rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}} |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.475889|N|136.73725|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine''' (大土御祖神社, *Ōtsuchi Mioya Jinja*) is a {{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|WD=Q1071160|lt=subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|1=Setsumatsusha}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}). This article also covers '''Uji no Nuki Shrine''' (宇治乃奴鬼神社, *Uji no Nuki Jinja*), which is co-located with Ōtsuchi Mioya Shrine and classified as an associated shrine of the Inner Shrine. Both shrines were established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p.61Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp.106–107. == Overview == Located at 2132 Osaki, Kusube-cho, Ise City, Mie Prefecture,Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.260 near {{ill|Isuzu Bridge|ja|五十鈴橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzu Bridge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106 The {{ill|Isuzu River|en|Isuzu River|WD=Q6089522|lt=Isuzu River|1=Isuzu River}} flows to the north of the shrine grounds.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.261 Deeper into the approach of {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社・宇治乃奴鬼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine & Ujino Nuki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, one will find {{ill|Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja|国津御祖神社・葦立弖神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Mioya Shrine & Ashidate Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.107 The shrine grounds are known as "Chaya no Mori" (Tea House Forest). At the entrance of the shrine grounds, there is a ditch about 1.5 meters wide.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.265 The two shrines share the same precinct and have a single {{ill|Shaden (main shrine building)|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaden (main shrine building)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} constructed in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} style, facing south and enclosed by a single {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=Tamagaki (sacred fence)|1=Sacred fences}}. No {{ill|Saisenbako (offertory box)|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisenbako (offertory box)|WD=Q135491444|1=Saisenbako}} is present. == Otsuchi Mioya Shrine == Ranked 10th among the 27 auxiliary shrines (sessha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-11 It has close ties with the {{ill|Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja|神宮神田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shinden (Ise Grand Shrine Rice Field)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.262 The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比古命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|水佐々良比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Ujino Nuki Shrine == Ranked 8th among the 16 auxiliary shrines (massha) of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.13-14 The shrine's old site is marked with a stone pillar. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Takamina Kami|ja|高水上命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamina Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, believed to be a deity of irrigation. == History == The shrine was established before the 5th year of Enchō (927), as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 == Rituals == Rituals such as {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai (Prayer Festival)|1=Kinensai}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai (Monthly Festival)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June, December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai (Harvest Festival)|1=Kannamesai Festival}} (October), and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai (First Fruits Festival)|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} (November) are held in front of the shrine.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.263 == Flora == The approach features large {{ill|Camphora officinarum|en|Camphora officinarum|WD=Q158722|lt=Camphor trees|1=Camphora officinarum}} and other significant trees like {{ill|Cryptomeria|en|Cryptomeria|WD=Q147388|lt=Cryptomeria (sugi)|1=Cryptomeria}} and {{ill|Torreya nucifera|en|Torreya nucifera|WD=Q1373999|lt=Japanese nutmeg-yew (kaya)|1=Torreya nucifera}}. == Access == Located near {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|en|Isuzugawa Station|WD=Q6089533|lt=Isuzugawa Station|1=Isuzugawa Station}}: * Approximately 13 minutes on foot (~1 km). * By {{ill|Ise City Community Bus|ja|伊勢市コミュニティバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City Community Bus|1=Q11379021|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379021}}, get off at "Shigō Elementary School" bus stop, 1-minute walk (~150 m). * About 1 minute (~750 m) east from the "Kusube" intersection via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 37|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357923}}. == References == * Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*.
